pattern: Counting pairs whose XOR lands in a range reduces to a prefix function f(x) = number of pairs with XOR strictly less than x, evaluated with a binary trie that, at each bit, can tally an entire subtree of partners in one step.
hints: Rewrite the range count as f(high+1) minus f(low), where f(x) counts pairs with XOR strictly below x; this removes the two-sided bound and leaves one monotone threshold.
Insert numbers into a binary trie keyed by their bits from most significant to least, storing how many numbers pass through each node.

To count partners giving XOR below x for a query number, walk the bits of x: when a bit of x is one you can add the whole subtree of partners that keep the XOR-prefix at zero there, then descend the branch that keeps the XOR equal to x's prefix.

edge_inputs.description: two equal elements XOR to zero which is below any low of at least one
two distinct elements with a tight range hitting their exact XOR
range excludes every pair so the answer is zero
low equals high so only an exact XOR value counts
duplicates with the widest range count every pair

stress.description: small n so the quadratic oracle stays fast, random values and range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 500))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 20000) for _ in range(n)]
    a = rng.randint(1, 20000)
    b = rng.randint(1, 20000)
    low, high = min(a, b), max(a, b)
    return [nums, low, high]

stress.description: small value alphabet to force many ties and repeated XORs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 500))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 16) for _ in range(n)]
    a = rng.randint(1, 31)
    b = rng.randint(1, 31)
    low, high = min(a, b), max(a, b)
    return [nums, low, high]
stress.note: dense collisions stress the trie counting
